summary.one_paragraph: The change improves ordering resilience with bounded retries and keeps scope focused on OrderProcessor. Tests cover failure paths. Minor nits on comment wording only.

overall_code_health.rationale: Clearer failure handling without widening module boundaries.

cl_description.rationale: Title and body state what, why, and idempotency tradeoff.

change_scope.rationale: Single feature with tests in the same change; four files is reviewable.

design.rationale: Retry policy lives in the processor; callers unchanged.

functionality.rationale: Backoff caps and idempotency align with stated intent.

complexity.rationale: Retry helper is small; processor method still readable.

tests.rationale: Tests assert exhaustion and success after transient errors.

comments.rationale: Why-backoff noted; one comment restates code.

consistency.rationale: Error types consistent with crate patterns.

documentation.rationale: README mentions retry behavior; ADR link in PR body.

context_and_review_depth.rationale: Fits ordering module; no cross-boundary leaks.

good_things: Idempotency requirement called out in the CL description before review.
Tests use a fake clock for backoff without sleeping in CI.

findings.observation: Comment '// retry with backoff' repeats the function name `retry_with_backoff`.
findings.why_it_matters: Comments should explain why, not restate identifiers (looking-for.md).
findings.recommended_action: Replace with why bounded backoff is used here or delete the comment.

limitations: Did not run integration tests against a live database.
suggested_next_steps: Optional: trim redundant comment on processor.rs:44 before merge.
